options.name: --token
options.description:
Appveyor token v2.
To enter your token, you have four options:
• Enter your token from your keyboard.
• Type a hyphen ('-'): This allows you to directly type or paste your token from your keyboard.
• Type '@' followed by the filename: This tells the system to read your token from the specified file.
• Ignore this option: If you don't enter anything, the system will try to automatically read your token from an environment variable or Windows Credential Manager: Available for Windows users only. This assumes you have already set up the token using config command.

options.name: --account
options.description: Appveyor User account: Optional. If not provided, the system will look for your account in the environment variables. This assumes you have already set up the account using config command.

options.name: --proxy-address
options.description: Proxy server should be in the form http://proxy:port

options.name: --proxy-user
options.description: Proxy user/password should be in the form 'username:password'

options.name: --what-if
options.description:
Run the command without executing the actions of the command so no changes occur.
It displays optins and argument values and the expected Http Request:
(Url, Method  <Get|Post|Put|Delete>, JsonBody. [default: False]

commands.name: artifacts
commands.description: Download build artifacts of last build or by a version in the project.

options.name: --version
options.description: Build version. Both job-id and version are mutually exclusive, either JobId or Version must be specified

options.name: --dir
options.description: Directory where to save artifacts, relative or absolute path [default: .]

commands.name: start
commands.description: Start build with more nested commands...

commands.name: commit
commands.description: Start build of specific branch commit.

options.name: --browse
options.description: Browse to the build page [default: False]

commands.name: config
commands.description:
Configure Appveyor token and account and allow storing token to Environment variables
or Windows Credential Manager (Windows users only).
Example 1: Storing token to Environment Variable
appvey config -a my-account -t my-token --action save
Example 2: Storing token to Windows Credential Manager
appvey config -a my-account -t my-token --action save --use-cred
Example 3: Show token store information
appvey config -a my-account --action info

options.name: --use-cred
options.description: Store token in Windows Credential Manager (Windows only). [default: False]

commands.name: http
commands.description:
Run Appveyor Rest Api Get/Post/Put/Delete.
Example 1: Get request
appvey /api/projects/my-account/my-project/history?recordsNumber=20
Example 2: Post request using json file
appvey -m post --json @file.json  /api/account/my-account/builds

options.name: --method
options.description: Http method but Case insensitive [default: Get]

commands.name: add
commands.description:
Add project.
Example 1: Add project for github
appvey project add -a my-account -t my-token my-account/my-project
Example 2: Add project for gitlab
appvey project add -a my-account -t my-token -p gitlab my-account/my-project

commands.name: build-version
commands.description:
Get project build by version.
Example 1:
appvey project build-version -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 1.2.3
Example 2:
appvey project bv -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project 1.2.3

commands.name: delete-cache
commands.description:
Delete project build cache.
Example 1:
appvey project delete-cache -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project
Example 2:
appvey project dc -a my-account -t my-token -s my-project

Example 2:

arguments.name: ENV
arguments.description:
Environment variables are defined in the format 'name:value'.
If the value is encrypted, please include 'true' after the value (e.g., 'name:value:true').
